label: Abuja
sub: Federal Capital Territory — purpose-built capital since 1991, home to National Assembly and Aso Rock Villa

label: Lagos
sub: Commercial capital and port city; population ~15 million; Nigeria's largest city and Africa's 2nd largest urban agglomeration

label: Kano
sub: Historic Hausa city-state; largest city in northern Nigeria; major commercial hub since medieval era; seat of Emir of Kano

label: Maiduguri
sub: Capital of Borno State; epicenter of Boko Haram/ISWAP insurgency since 2009; headquarters of Multinational Joint Task Force

label: Port Harcourt
sub: Oil industry capital; Rivers State; home to NNPC refineries and Shell Nigeria headquarters; epicenter of Niger Delta conflict

label: Enugu
sub: Capital of Biafra 1967–1970; now capital of Enugu State; symbolic center of Igbo political identity

label: Ibadan
sub: Largest Yoruba city; seat of University of Ibadan (Nigeria's first university, 1948); home of Action Group nationalism

label: Sokoto
sub: Seat of the Sokoto Caliphate (1809–1903); Sultan of Sokoto is spiritual leader of Nigerian Muslims; northwestern Nigeria

label: Kaduna
sub: Major northern city; site of recurrent Christian-Muslim communal violence (2000, 2002, 2011); 'Middle Belt' flashpoint

label: Benin City
sub: Capital of the Benin Kingdom; home of Oba of Benin; source of the Benin Bronzes looted by British in 1897; Edo State capital

label: Ile-Ife
sub: Sacred cradle of Yoruba civilization; seat of the Ooni of Ife; famous for naturalistic bronze and terracotta royal portraits (~1000 CE)

label: Calabar
sub: Old Calabar; former capital of Nigeria's Eastern protectorate; slave-trade entrepôt; site of Mary Slessor's mission; Cross River State

label: Lokoja
sub: First British administrative capital of Nigeria (1900); at confluence of Niger and Benue rivers; Royal Niger Company HQ

label: Badagry
sub: Major transatlantic slave trade port; 'Point of No Return'; site of Brazilian returnee communities; historic slave route museum

label: Chibok
sub: Site of April 2014 Boko Haram mass kidnapping of 276 schoolgirls; sparked global #BringBackOurGirls campaign; Borno State

label: Sambisa Forest
sub: Boko Haram / ISWAP stronghold; declared 'caliphate' by Shekau 2014; remote game reserve used as terrorist base and hostage site

label: Ogoniland
sub: Home of Ogoni people; site of Ken Saro-Wiwa's MOSOP movement; shell oil spills declared emergency by UNEP 2011; Rivers State

label: Warri
sub: Delta State oil city; epicenter of 1997–2003 Itsekiri-Ijaw-Urhobo communal conflict and MEND militancy; offshore oil terminal hub

label: Nok Heritage Site
sub: Archaeological site of the Nok culture (~500 BCE–200 CE); earliest iron-smelting and terracotta sculpture in sub-Saharan Africa; Kaduna State

label: Zaria
sub: Ancient Hausa emirate; Ahmadu Bello University (northern Nigeria's largest); site of 1987 Kafanchan and 1992 Zango-Kataf religious riots

label: Onitsha
sub: Largest inland market in Africa; key Igbo commercial city on the Niger River; pivotal in Biafran War as frontline city

label: Daura
sub: Mythic birthplace of Hausa Bakwai city-states; hometown of President Buhari; ancient emirate in Katsina State

label: Katsina
sub: Historic Hausa emirate; President Buhari's home state; target of Boko Haram attacks and banditry; Kankara schoolboys kidnapping 2020

label: Bonny Island
sub: Nigeria LNG export terminal and Bonny Light crude export facility; one of Africa's largest LNG facilities; Rivers State

label: Baga
sub: Site of January 2015 Boko Haram massacre; estimated 2,000 civilians killed in worst single Boko Haram attack; Lake Chad shore, Borno State
